author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VII. 19–24.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The passage of the holy Gospel of which we have before discoursed to you, beloved, is followed by that of to-day, which has just now been read.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both the disciples and the Jews heard the Lord speaking; both men of truth and liars heard the Truth speaking; both friends and enemies heard Charity speaking; both good men and bad men heard the Good speaking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They heard, but He discerned; He saw and foresaw whom His discourse profited and would profit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among those who were then, He saw; among us who were to be, He foresaw.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us therefore hear the Gospel, just as if we were listening to the Lord Himself present: nor let us say, O happy they who were able to see Him! because there were many of them who saw, and also killed Him; and there are many among us who have not seen Him, and yet have believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the precious truth that sounded forth from the mouth of the Lord was both written for our sakes, and preserved for our sakes, and recited for our sakes, and will be recited also for the sake of our prosperity, even until the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord is above; but the Lord, the Truth, is also here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the body of the Lord, in which He rose again from the dead, can be only in one place; but His truth is everywhere diffused.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then hear the Lord, and let us also speak that which He shall have granted to us concerning His own words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Did not Moses,” saith He, “give you the law, and yet none of you doeth the law?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do ye seek to kill me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For ye seek to kill me just for this reason, that none of you doeth the law; for if ye did do the law, ye would recognize Christ in its very letters, and ye would not kill Him when present with you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they answered: “The crowd answered Him;” answered as a tumultuous crowd, things not pertaining to order, but to confusion; in a word, the crowd was disturbed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what answer it made: “Thou hast a devil: who seeks to kill thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if it were not worse to say, “Thou hast a devil,” than to kill Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To Him, indeed, was it said, that He had a devil, who was casting out devils.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What else can a turbulent disorderly crowd say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What else can filth stirred up do but stink?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The crowd was disturbed; by what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the eyes that have not soundness cannot endure the brightness of the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord, manifestly not disturbed, but calm in His truth, rendered not evil for evil nor railing for railing; although, if He were to say to these men, You have a devil, He would certainly be saying what was true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they would not have said such things to the Truth, unless the falsehood of the devil had instigated them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then did He answer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us calmly hear, and drink in the serene word: “I have done one work, and ye all marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if He said, What if ye were to see all my works?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they were His works which they saw in the world, and yet they saw not Him who made them all: He did one thing, and they were disturbed because he made a man whole on the Sabbath-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if, indeed, when any sick man recovered his health on the Sabbath-day, it had been any other that made such a man whole than He who offended them, because He made one man whole on the Sabbath-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who else has made others whole than He who is health itself,—He who gives even to the beasts that health which He gave to this man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was bodily health.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The health of the flesh is repaired, and the flesh dies; and when it is repaired, death is only put off, not taken away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: However, even that same health, brethren, is from the Lord, through whomsoever it may be given: by whose care and ministry soever it may be imparted, it is given by Him from whom all health is, to whom it is said in the psalm, “O Lord, Thou wilt save men and beasts; as Thou hast multiplied Thy mercy, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For because Thou art God Thy multiplied mercy reaches even to the safety of human flesh, reaches even to the safety of dumb animals; but Thou who givest health of flesh common to men and beasts, is there no health which Thou reservest for men?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is certainly another which is not only not common to men and beasts, but to men themselves is not common to good and bad.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, when he had there spoken of this health which men and cattle receive in common, because of that health which men, but only the good, ought to hope for, he added as he went on: “But the sons of men shall put their trust under the cover of Thy wings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They shall be fully satisfied with the fatness of Thy house; and Thou shalt give them drink from the torrent of Thy pleasure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For with Thee is the fountain of life; and in Thy light shall they see light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the health which belongs to good men, those whom he called “sons of men;” whilst he had said above, “O Lord, Thou shall save men and beasts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were not those men sons of men, that after he had said men, he should go on and say, But the sons of men: as if men and sons of men meant different things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet I do not believe that the Holy Spirit had said this without some indication of distinction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The term men refers to the first Adam, sons of men to Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps, indeed, men relate to the first man; but sons of men relate to the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have done one work, and ye all marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And immediately He subjoined: “Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was well done that ye received circumcision from Moses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers;” since it was Abraham that first received circumcision from the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And ye circumcise on the Sabbath-day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moses has convicted you: ye have received in the law to circumcise on the eighth day; ye have received in the law to cease from labor on the seventh day; if the eighth day from the child’s birth fall on the seventh day of the week, what will ye do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Will ye abstain from work to keep the Sabbath, or will ye circumcise to fulfill the sacrament of the eighth day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I know, saith He, what ye do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye circumcise a man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because circumcision relates to what is a kind of seal of salvation, and men ought not to abstain from the work of salvation on the Sabbath-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore be ye not “angry with me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the Sabbath-day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If,” saith He, “a man on the Sabbath-day receiveth circumcision that the law should not be broken” (for it was something saving that was ordained by Moses in that ordinance of circumcision), why are ye angry at me for working a healing on the Sabbath-day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps, indeed, that circumcision pointed to the Lord Himself, at whom they were indignant, because He worked cures and healing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For circumcision was commanded to be applied on the eighth day: and what is circumcision but the spoiling of the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This circumcision, then, signified the removal of carnal lusts from the heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore not without cause was it given, and ordered to be made in that member; since by that member the creature of mortal kind is procreated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By one man came death, just as by one man the resurrection of the dead; and by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore every man is born with a foreskin, because every man is born with the vice of propagation; and God cleanses not, either from the vice with which we are born, or from the vices which we add thereto by ill living, except by the stony knife, the Lord Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ was the Rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now they used to circumcise with stone knives, and by the name of rock they prefigured Christ; and yet when He was present with them they did not acknowledge Him, but besides, they sought to kill Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why on the eighth day, unless because after the seventh day of the week the Lord rose again on the Lord’s day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore Christ’s resurrection, which happened on the third day indeed of His passion, but on the eighth day in the days of the week, that same resurrection it is that doth circumcise us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear of those that were circumcised with the real stone, while the apostle admonishes them: “If then ye be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ is, sitting on the right hand of God; set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He speaks to the circumcised: Christ has risen; He has taken away from you carnal desires, evil lusts, the superfluity with which you were born, and that far worse which you had added thereto by ill living; being circumcised by the Rock, why do you still set your affections on the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And finally, for that “Moses gave you the law, and ye circumcise a man on the Sabbath-day,” understand that by this is signified the good work which I have done, in that I have made a man every whit whole on the Sabbath-day; because he was cured that he might be whole in body, and also he believed that he might be whole in soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Judge not according to personal appearance, but judge righteous judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just now, you who by the law of Moses circumcise on the Sabbath-day are not angry with Moses; and because I made a man whole on the Sabbath-day you are angry with me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You judge by the person; give heed to the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do not prefer myself to Moses, says the Lord, who was also the Lord of Moses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So consider us as you would two men, as both men; judge between us, but judge a true judgment; do not condemn him by honoring me, but honor me by understanding him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this He said to them in another place: “If ye believed Moses ye would certainly believe me also, for he wrote of me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in this place He willed not to say this, Himself and Moses being as it were placed before these men for judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because of Moses’ law you circumcise, even when it happens to be the Sabbath-day, and will ye not that I should show the beneficence of healing during the Sabbath?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord of circumcision and the Lord of the Sabbath is the same who is the Author of health; and they are servile works that ye are forbidden to do on the Sabbath; if ye really understand what servile works are, ye sin not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he that committeth sin is the servant of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it a servile work to heal a man on the Sabbath-day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye do eat and drink (to infer somewhat from the admonition of our Lord Jesus Christ, and from His words); at any rate, why do ye eat and drink on the Sabbath, but because that what ye do pertains to health?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this ye show that the works of health are not in any wise to be omitted on the Sabbath.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “do not judge by person, but judge righteous judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider me as ye would a man; consider Moses as a man: if ye will judge according to the truth, ye will condemn neither Moses nor me; and when ye know the truth ye will know me, because I am the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It requires great labor in this world, brethren to get clear of the vice which the Lord has noted in this place, so as not to judge by appearance, but to keep right judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, indeed, admonished the Jews, but He warned us also; them He convicted, us He instructed; them He reproved, us He encouraged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not imagine that this was not said to us, simply because we were not there at that time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was written, it is read; when it was recited we heard it; but we heard it as said to the Jews; let us not place ourselves behind ourselves and watch Him reproving enemies, while we ourselves do that which the truth may reprove in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews indeed judged by appearance, but for that reason they belong not to the New Testament, they have not the kingdom of heaven in Christ, nor are joined to the society of the holy angels; they sought earthly things of the Lord; for a land of promise, victory over enemies, fruitfulness of child-bearing, increase of children, abundance of fruit,—all which things were indeed promised to them by God, the True and the Good, promised to them, however, as unto carnal men,—all these things made for them the Old Testament.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the Old Testament?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The inheritance, as it were, belonging to the old man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have been renewed, have been made a new man, because He who is the new man has come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is so new as to be born of a virgin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, because there was not in Him what instruction might renew, because He had no sin, there was given Him a new origin of birth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In Him a new birth, in us a new man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is a new man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A man renewed from oldness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Renewed unto what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Unto desiring heavenly things, unto longing for things eternal, unto earnestly seeking the country which is above and fears no foe, where we do not lose a friend nor fear an enemy; where we live with good affection, without any want; where no longer any advances, because none fails; where no man is born, because no man dies; where there is no hungering nor thirsting; where immortality is fullness, and truth our aliment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Having these promises, and pertaining to the New Testament, and being made heirs of a new inheritance, and co-heirs of the Lord Himself, we have a far different hope from theirs: let us not judge by appearance, but hold right judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is he that judges not according to the person?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that loves equally.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Equal love causes that persons be not accepted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not when we honor men in diverse measure according to their degrees that we ought to fear lest we are accepting persons.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where we judge between two, and at times between relations, sometimes it happens that judgment has to be made between father and son; the father complains of a bad son, or the son complains of a harsh father; we regard the honor which is due to the father from the son; we do not make the son equal to the father in honor, but we give him preference if he has a good cause: let us regard the son on an equality with the father in the truth, and thus shall we bestow the honor due, so that equity destroy not merit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus we profit by the words of the Lord, and that we may profit, we are assisted by His grace.
